history.content:
<thought>
I am on the left side of the map facing right. The goal `O` is below and to the right, and the immediate cell ahead is lava `L`, so moving forward would be unsafe. I need to turn away from the lava and find a safe route downward toward the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the left side of the map on the second row, facing down. The goal `O` is far to the lower-right, and moving forward would go into lava, so I should first turn right to face east and then head along the safe open path toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am on the second row near the left side and facing left (`<`). The goal `O` is on the lower-right area, so I need to start heading right. Turning left from left-facing will make me face down, which is not helpful because the space below is lava/wall-heavy. I should turn right to face up? Wait, from left-facing, turning right makes me face up, so I can reassess and move toward the open corridor safely.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the upper-left area facing up, with a wall directly ahead, so moving forward is impossible. The goal `O` is in the lower-right area, and the open path appears to go right along the top row, so I should turn right to face east.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am on the left side of the map facing right (`>`). The goal `O` is below and to the right, and the cell immediately ahead is lava, so moving forward is unsafe. I should turn to face downward so I can move around the obstacle toward the lower open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the left side of the map facing down. The cells directly below are walls soon, so I need to move toward the goal located near the bottom-right. The safest first step is to turn right to face the open path along the top row, avoiding lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am on the left side of the map facing left (`<`). The cell directly ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. The goal `O` is far to the right and slightly down, so I should first turn to face right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the upper-left area, facing up, with lava immediately to my right and a clear path along the lower corridor to the goal `O` near the bottom-right. I need to head downward first, so I should turn right to face east? Wait: from facing up, turning right faces right, but the top row is blocked by lava and walls, so I should instead turn around toward down by turning right twice; however, a single turn is the only action now, so I will start by turning right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am on the top row near the left side, facing right. The cell immediately ahead is lava, so I cannot move forward safely. To avoid the lava and work my way toward the goal in the lower-right area, I should turn downward first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the left side of the map facing down, with lava immediately ahead if I move forward. The goal is on the lower-right, so I should avoid the lava-filled column and first turn right to head toward the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am on the left side facing left, with a wall immediately to my left, so moving forward is not useful. The goal is to the lower right, and the open path is to my right, so I should turn to face right first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at the left side facing down, and the path to the goal is along the open bottom-right area. Moving forward would be unsafe because there is lava ahead, so I should turn right to face into the corridor of open cells.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the left side of the second row facing left (`<`), and there is a wall directly to my left, so moving forward is blocked. The goal `O` is far to the lower right, and the open path begins by moving right, so I should turn to face right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the upper-left area facing up. The cell ahead is a wall, so moving forward is impossible. I need to head toward the open path and eventually down to the goal at the bottom-right. Turning right will face me toward the safer open corridor on the right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the left side of the top open corridor, facing right. The goal `O` is near the bottom-right, and the direct path to the right is blocked by lava in the top rows, so I should go downward into the clear passage first. To face down from right, I need to turn clockwise.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am on the left side of the map facing down, and the cells directly below and ahead are blocked by walls or lava, so I need to reorient to move along the safe open corridor toward the goal at the lower right. The safest first step is to turn left to face right, where there is an open path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the left side of the map, facing right. The path ahead is blocked by lava, so I need to go downward first along the safe corridor to reach the bottom row and then move right toward the goal. The next safe step is to turn downward.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am on the left side facing down, and there is lava immediately to my right and more open space below. The goal `O` is on the lower-right area, so the safest first step is to turn left to face right, then I can move along the open top corridor toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the left side of the map facing right. The goal is on the lower-right area. Moving forward now would immediately hit lava at the next cell, so I need to adjust my path to avoid the lava columns and head downward first. Turning right will face me downward.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am near the left side facing down, with lava immediately ahead if I move forward. The goal is on the lower-right area, so I should first turn right to face toward the open corridor and then proceed toward the goal while avoiding lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am on the second row, facing left (`<`), with lava immediately ahead, so I should not move forward. The goal `O` is in the lower-right area, so I need to turn to face downward or rightward and navigate around the lava columns. Turning left from left-facing will make me face down.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am near the left side facing down. The goal `O` is in the bottom row toward the right, and the path ahead is blocked by lava, so I need to first head right along the open corridor. Turning left from down will make me face right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am on the second row near the left, facing right. The path immediately ahead is a lava cell, so I should avoid moving forward into it. To head toward the goal near the lower-right, I need to go down first, which means turning right to face downward.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the left side of the map facing down, and the path directly below is blocked by lava or walls. The goal is to the lower-right, so I need to start moving right along the open corridor. First I should turn left to face right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the left side of the second row, facing right. The goal `O` is on the bottom-right area, and there is a clear path by moving right across the top corridor until I can head down toward the goal. The next safe step is to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
